flagged_messages.message_text: ROW-4609: '$180 due' | ROW-4610: 'And $1000 due next Wednesday' | ROW-4611: 'Also you're late on daycare' | ROW-4612: 'And don't forget about the $1k due next Wednesday' | ROW-4613: 'Beginning this year through my work I'm no longer required to pay a retainer so it's easier to take you to court if needed'
flagged_messages.primary_reasoning: FIRST litigation-capability escalation disclosure. Melissa announces that her employer benefits now cover legal fees without a retainer, making litigation 'easier.' ADD-002 temporal rule: first instance of a new threat sub-category = Sev-2. This is qualitatively different from prior legal threats: prior threats asserted intent to use legal mechanisms; this message announces that the practical barrier to doing so has been removed. The retainer-free access materially increases the credibility of all prior and future legal threats — litigation is now lower-cost for Melissa. Context: the disclosure immediately follows three successive payment-pressure messages (ROW-4609-4612) about $180 and $1,000 due — it functions as an amplifier of those payment demands. 'If needed' is a mild hedge that doesn't reduce the threat value; the sentence still functions as a compliance warning. For the GAL: this is a structural change in the threat environment that occurred in early 2024 and should be understood as elevating the credibility of all subsequent legal threats.
flagged_messages.context_notes: The disclosure appears sandwiched between payment pressure messages and is followed by a logistics exchange (ROW-4614-4616). The casual insertion of the capability disclosure into a payment-demand sequence makes it more threatening, not less — it reads as 'by the way, in case you're thinking of not paying, it just got cheaper for me to pursue you.'
flagged_messages.quote_excerpt: 'Beginning this year through my work I'm no longer required to pay a retainer so it's easier to take you to court if needed' (ROW-4613)

flagged_messages.message_text: ROW-4663: 'Remember to pay the $1000 by end of today. Otherwise you face 30 days jail'
flagged_messages.primary_reasoning: FIRST explicit imprisonment-duration threat. Prior financial threat language used jail framing generically ('about to go to jail' ROW-2171 B-006). ROW-4663 is the first message specifying a concrete jail duration: '30 days.' The specificity of '30 days' elevates this beyond generic consequence framing — it represents a precise legal penalty calculation, suggesting Melissa has consulted her attorney about the specific consequence or researched it. Coming just five days after the retainer-free disclosure (ROW-4613, Feb 23), the 30-day jail threat operates in a newly elevated threat environment: Melissa has just announced easier access to court, and now she specifies the exact imprisonment consequence of non-compliance. The sequence ROW-4613 → ROW-4663 (Feb 23 → Feb 28, 5 days) is the most compressed and escalatory legal-threat sequence in the dataset to this point. ADD-002: first instance of explicit-imprisonment-duration category = Sev-2.
flagged_messages.context_notes: The $1,000 payment referenced is the monthly attorney fees payment from the court-order or agreement (per ROW-4364 B-012). Context: ROW-4662 Eli was preparing for a meeting when the jail threat arrived. The threat is delivered with no prior warning in the exchange — an isolated demand message. Eli's response is not captured in this batch (the following messages are unrelated logistics). The absence of a compliance-response message in immediate follow-up may suggest the payment was made silently, or was late.
flagged_messages.quote_excerpt: 'Remember to pay the $1000 by end of today. Otherwise you face 30 days jail' (ROW-4663)

unflagged_notable.assessment: RF-04 contempt (COMP-RF04-012 per RF-04 pass: child-relay guilt + extreme slur). RF-04+RF-05 compound per RF-04 pass — RF-05 role is aggressive-tone peripheral (Sev-1) within the RF-04 primary compound. Not RF-05 primary in this pass.

arc_state_snapshot.note: ARC-RF05-001 ACTIVE. B-013 adds two Sev-2 signals (S-013 and S-014): litigation-capability escalation (ROW-4613) and first explicit 30-day imprisonment threat (ROW-4663). Both are first instances of new sub-categories. The Feb 23→Feb 28 sequence is the dataset's most compressed escalatory legal-threat sequence to date.

items: CP3-RF05-001 (MEDIUM): ROW-4613 (Feb 23 2024) — retainer-free court access disclosure. Attorney should verify whether Melissa's employer legal benefit is confirmed by any other record (discovery, deposition). If confirmed, this represents a structural change in the threat environment that elevates all Phase B and C legal threats. Eli: do you recall if Melissa ever mentioned her employer or work benefits in the context of legal access before this message?

batch_observations.tone_assessment: B-013 (Feb-Mar 2024) is the highest-severity RF-05 batch to date: two Sev-2 signals in 5 days, with the retainer-free disclosure materially elevating the legal-threat environment before the explicit jail threat arrives. The arc is intensifying. The paper-tiger pattern (ADD-002) may be partially disrupted by the retainer-free access — the barrier to follow-through has been reduced.
batch_observations.cross_rf_context: RF-04 B-013: COMP-RF04-012 ('Such a worthless dick' + child guilt relay) at ROW-4793-4800. RF-05 peripheral for 'such a worthless dick.' Also: RF-04 pass CP3-RF04-013 notes ROW-4587-4588 (B-013) — NC relocation mention (RF-02). Relocation signal in same batch as legal-capability escalation is worth noting for synthesis.
